name: Joey Baron
abstract: Bernard Joseph Baron (born June 26, 1955 in Richmond, Virginia) is an American drummer best-known for working in avant-garde jazz with Bill Frisell and John Zorn.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: From Spirituals to Swing
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: Right now I’m trying to do some stuff with Joey Baron who played on my last record.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: He played, but we played together and that was my group—Joey Baron
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Soph
about_quotes.evidence: I only know of one drummer who likes playing on cymbals he’s never played on before. He’s a great drummer, a great musician, Joey Baron.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Soph interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 9, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I know how each one of those guys plays—Greg Osby and Steve (LaSpina?) and Joey Baron.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Magnusson
about_quotes.evidence: I really want to use Joey Baron... I talked to Joey a little bit about it, and he’s interested. And Peter Sprague... and do a quartet thing. So that looks like it might happen.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Magnusson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
